paper_title: MergeMix: A Unified Augmentation Paradigm for Visual and Multi-Modal Understanding

limitations.SFT: Depends on high-quality annotations, doesn't model relative preferences
limitations.RL: Requires additional reward model that may encode bias or be exploitable

previous_work_issues.SeVa: Highly random augmentations, DPO loss not related to data, cannot control quality of loser samples

key_components.description: Generates mixed samples through token merge techniques
features: Bipartite soft matching for similarity information
Links merge ratio and mixing ratio

key_components.description: Builds preference pairs without reward models

challenges_addressed: Optimal trade-off between efficiency and performance of mixup augmentations
Moving from classical image corruptions to data-dependent samples
benefits: Preserves stability and scalability of SFT
Direct relationship between optimization object and augmentation

results.performance: Competitive performance in both image classification and multi-modal tasks

title: MergeMix: A Unified Augmentation Paradigm for Visual and Multi-Modal Understanding
